content.verdict: Iloha Seaview Hotel is the most charming mid-luxury honeymoon address on Réunion's west coast — 65 hillside bungalows in traditional Creole style, scattered through tropical gardens above the Indian Ocean at Saint-Leu. The view from the property is among the great vistas of the island: an unbroken Indian Ocean panorama framed by flame trees and coconut palms, with the surf breaking on the legendary left-hand reef of Saint-Leu below. For honeymooners, Iloha hits a sweet spot the larger five-star resorts can't quite match — the bungalows feel private and storybook-pretty, the price point is markedly more accessible, and the location places you directly above the reef where humpback whales pass within sight of the breakfast terrace from June to October. The honeymoon angle is the combination of authentic Creole architecture, a quieter west-coast village atmosphere than Saint-Gilles, and the property's status as the unofficial gateway to whale-watching season. Couples who prioritise charm over scale, who like the idea of sleeping in a stand-alone bungalow rather than a hotel block, and who want a real sense of being on a French overseas department rather than at a generic Indian Ocean resort find Iloha quietly exceptional. It is also the most natural base on the island for couples interested in surfing, whale-watching, and the dramatic west-coast cliffs.
content.best_room: Book a Bungalow Vue Mer Premium for the honeymoon — a stand-alone Creole-style bungalow with private terrace, four-poster bed, and an unbroken Indian Ocean panorama. The Suite Familiale Duplex Vue Mer offers more space across two levels with the same defining view. Hillside-position bungalows enjoy the longest sightlines; the property arranges premium ocean-view bungalows on request.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Sunset on the Terrace
itinerary_7_nights.description: Roland Garros Airport is around 70 minutes from Saint-Leu. Arrive at Iloha and walk up through tropical gardens to your bungalow. The west-coast sunset over the Indian Ocean from a private terrace is the defining first impression of staying here. Dinner at Le Lagon des Filaos restaurant introduces the Creole-French menu.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Whale Watching Boat Trip (June-October)
itinerary_7_nights.description: Saint-Leu is the prime whale-watching village on Réunion — humpback mothers and calves pass within metres of the boats during peak season. A 3-hour morning trip departs from the nearby harbour. Outside whale season, swap this day for a guided snorkel at the Saint-Leu marine reserve, one of the richest reef sections on the island.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Piton de la Fournaise Volcano
itinerary_7_nights.description: Réunion's active volcano is around 2 hours by car from Saint-Leu. The drive up through the Plaine des Sables — a high-altitude desert of red ash — is otherworldly. Hike to the rim of Dolomieu crater (2-3 hours return). Bring layers; the altitude exceeds 2,500 metres. Return to Iloha for a hot bath in the bungalow and an early dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cirque de Cilaos Day Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: The famous 400-turn road climbs into the Cirque de Cilaos — a hidden volcanic valley of vineyards, lentil fields, and embroidery shops. Lunch on a Creole table d'hôte. The drive is slow but the landscape is among the most spectacular in the Indian Ocean.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cirque de Salazie & Hell-Bourg
itinerary_7_nights.description: Salazie is the wettest and lushest of the three cirques, with waterfalls cascading off every cliff. The village of Hell-Bourg is officially classified among France's most beautiful villages. Visit a colonial-era spa house and lunch on cari poulet. Return to Iloha for sunset cocktails on the terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Lagoon Snorkeling at L'Hermitage & Spa Afternoon
itinerary_7_nights.description: The L'Hermitage and Saline-les-Bains lagoons north of Saint-Leu are the safest swimming beaches on the west coast — drive 20 minutes north and spend the morning on the protected sand. Lunch at a beach restaurant, then return for an afternoon couples ritual at the Iloha spa.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Breakfast Above the Reef
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final breakfast on the terrace with the ocean spread out below, then the drive back to Roland Garros. The hotel can arrange a private transfer; many couples stop in Saint-Paul on the way to visit the colourful Friday seafront market.

caveats: Réunion's west coast has a serious shark warning — swimming and surfing outside the protected lagoons is prohibited at most beaches, including the surf reef directly below Iloha. The nearest fully safe swimming lagoons are at L'Hermitage and Saline-les-Bains, around 20 minutes north by car.
Cyclone season runs January through March — risk of flight disruption and reduced outdoor activity. May through October is the prime honeymoon and whale-watching window.
The bungalows climb the hillside — guests with mobility limitations should request a lower-level bungalow at booking. The walk between bungalows and restaurant involves stairs and slopes.
Flights from outside Europe are routed almost exclusively through Paris (11 hours from CDG) or Mauritius. North American couples should plan a stopover.

faqs.question: Is Iloha Seaview Hotel good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — the stand-alone Creole bungalows, ocean-panorama terraces, and quieter Saint-Leu setting make it a genuinely romantic base. Iloha offers a particularly good combination of charm and value compared with the larger Saint-Gilles resorts, and is the natural choice for couples drawn to whale-watching season.

faqs.question: Réunion vs Mauritius for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Mauritius offers a longer-established lagoon-resort circuit. Réunion offers an active volcano, three volcanic cirques, French overseas department culture, and far fewer tourists. Iloha in particular suits couples who like the idea of a Creole hillside bungalow above the reef rather than a sand-front hotel block.

faqs.question: When is the best time to see whales?
faqs.answer: Humpback whale season runs June through October, with peak sightings in August and September. Saint-Leu is the most consistent whale-watching village on the island and Iloha sits directly above the boat departure point.

faqs.question: Can we swim at the beach below the hotel?
faqs.answer: No — the reef break directly below Iloha is famous for surfing but not safe for swimming due to Réunion's shark warning. The protected swimming lagoons of L'Hermitage and Saline-les-Bains are around 20 minutes north by car and offer excellent calm-water snorkeling.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, Iloha welcomes families, but the bungalow layout, dispersed property design, and terrace dining keep the atmosphere quieter and more couple-friendly than a typical resort. Outside French school holidays, couples make up a large share of the guest mix.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to Réunion's Roland Garros Airport (RUN) — direct flights from Paris with Air France, Air Austral, French Bee, and Corsair. The drive to Saint-Leu takes around 70 minutes; the hotel arranges private transfers.
